article_meta.title: News Law & the Courts Black Lives Matter Director Spent Millions in Donations on Homes, Shopping, Vacations, Indictment

reasoning: The model does a good job overall. It correctly identifies bias in the author's own prose and avoids flagging quoted text. The three instances flagged are all defensible. 'Personal indulgences' is indeed loaded author prose (Word Choice is appropriate). 'Setting off a spending spree' with 'very little oversight' is author framing that goes beyond neutral reporting (Slant is reasonable). The 'only one third' observation is a fair catch — the word 'only' is editorializing by the author. There are minor quibbles possible but no attribution rule violations. Some might argue there are additional bias instances that could have been caught (e.g., the headline framing, the structural pattern of linking multiple BLM-related fraud cases to create a broader narrative pattern), but the model was appropriately cautious.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged items are from the author's own prose. No quoted text was flagged. The model correctly avoided flagging the indictment allegations or attributed statements.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three items are defensible. 'Personal indulgences' is clearly loaded author language. 'Setting off a spending spree' is interpretive framing. 'Only one third' uses the editorializing word 'only.' These are all genuine bias instances in the author's voice.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed some potential instances: the headline's framing ('Spent Millions... on Homes, Shopping, Vacations' front-loads negative characterization), the structural slant of devoting the second half of the article to cataloguing other BLM fraud cases (creating a pattern narrative beyond the specific indictment), and potentially 'Donations began pouring in' as slightly loaded. The phrase 'the murder of George Floyd' is notable as an opinion statement presented as fact since Floyd's death was legally a murder conviction but calling it 'murder' in journalistic prose can be debated. However, missing 1-2 structural bias instances is understandable.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Word Choice for 'personal indulgences' is correct. Slant for the spending spree paragraph is reasonable though Spin might also work. Slant for 'only one third' is reasonable — it's about framing emphasis. The labels are defensible if not perfectly precise in every case.
